sources.notes: Statement and constraints: answer capped-card maximum-sum queries over two arrays.
Official solution provides both merged top-z repair and ternary-search variants.
Independent write-up explains prefix sums and the unimodal ternary-search alternative.

annotation.summary: Given two arrays and many queries (x,y,z), choose exactly z numbers with at most x from a and at most y from b, maximizing the sum.
annotation.constraints: Across tests, sum n,m <= 200000 and sum q <= 100000; after sorting, each query should be O(1) or O(log n).
annotation.core_idea: Sort each array descending and build prefix sums. Without caps, the best z cards are obtained by merging the two sorted arrays; precompute for every z how many come from each side. For a query, if the unrestricted top-z choice respects both caps, use it. If it exceeds x from a, the optimum is forced to take exactly x from a and z-x from b; symmetrically if it exceeds y from b. Prefix sums then answer immediately.
annotation.complexity: O((n+m) log(n+m) + q) per test case for the merged solution
tricks: Precompute composition of the global top z cards.
A violated cap pins the number chosen from that side.
Prefix sums make fixed-count best sums O(1).

solution_variants.name: merged-top-z-repair
solution_variants.summary: Sort a and b, precompute prefix sums and the pair (count_from_a,count_from_b) in the unrestricted best z-card merge; repair only the violated cap per query.

solution_variants.name: unimodal-ternary-search
solution_variants.summary: For each query maximize pa[i]+pb[z-i] over max(0,z-y) <= i <= min(z,x); the function is unimodal because both arrays are sorted.

tags.evidence: For any fixed count from each array, taking the largest elements is optimal, and the unrestricted global top-z selection only needs cap repair.
Both the prefix sums and the global top-z composition depend on sorting the two arrays by value.
After sorting, each fixed count answer is computed as pa[cnt_a]+pb[cnt_b].
The primary solution precomputes the unrestricted best composition for every z by merging two sorted arrays.
The alternate official solution uses ternary search on the unimodal function pa[i]+pb[z-i].
